mappings.rationaleAI: Capacity for abstraction, reasoning, and adaptive behavior constituting intellectual ability (D), with IQ and general factor g central to intelligence distribution (E), including debates about standards (E.1), intellectual disability (E.2), giftedness (E.3), and group differences across age, class, race, and sex (E.4).
Behavioral, cognitive, and emotional patterns constituting characteristic adjustment, encompassing human capacities (A) and personal propensities and idiosyncrasies affecting behavior (F).
Scientific study of mind and behavior encompassing human capacities (A), psychological measurement (B), theories of intelligence (D), and attitude research (G).
Neurodevelopmental disorder defined by IQ below 70 with adaptive behavior deficits, directly constituting the topic of intellectual retardation (E.2).

Discipline of data analysis including sampling design and inferential methods, providing the methodological foundation for standardized test development (B.2), assessment of test results (B.3), and measurement of attitudes through questionnaires and sampling techniques (G.4).
Cognitive processes including reasoning, concept formation, and problem-solving, relevant to the nature of human capacities (A) and theories of intelligence as higher cognitive ability (D).
Acquisition of knowledge and skills through experience and instruction, fundamental to the nature of human capacities and their development (A).
Conditions limiting activity or participation, with intellectual and developmental disability directly relevant to retardation as a category of intelligence distribution (E.2).

Beliefs about heritable racial differences in behavioral traits directly concern the measurement and interpretation of race-based group differences in intelligence (E.4), and racism functions as a prejudicial attitude (G).
Social and psychological dimensions of gender relate to sex differences in intelligence measurement (E.4) and gendered attitudes as socially constructed inclinations (G).
Hierarchical stratification by economic position and education directly relevant to socioeconomic class differences in intelligence measurement and interpretation (E.4).
Subjective mental attitude toward propositions, central to the nature of attitudes (G.1), with belief formation through perception, inference, and emotion relevant to attitude development (G.3).
Unjustified distinctions based on group membership, functioning as enacted attitudes (G) serving defensive or adjustive functions (G.2).
Transmission of knowledge and skills relates to developing human capacities (A), and educational factors shape socioeconomic group differences in intelligence measures (E.4).
Cognitive capacity for logical processing through deductive and inductive reasoning, constituting a core intellectual ability (D) and dimension of human capacities (A).
Enduring patterns of sexual identity and orientation as personal propensities and idiosyncrasies affecting behavior and experience (F).
Socio-political movement critiquing patriarchal attitudes (G), examining the social functions of gendered attitudes (G.2), and seeking to reshape attitude development regarding gender (G.3).
Psychoanalytic theory of unconscious drives, id-ego-superego structure, and personality development, addressing the nature of human capacities (A) and personal propensities affecting behavior (F).
Intentional use of force reflecting persistent aggressive propensities and idiosyncrasies that influence behavior and experience (F).
